key_insight: Replacing the DFTB repulsive pair potential with a many-body MACE correction creates a DFTB+MACE model that is more accurate than standard DFTB while still retaining explicit electronic structure information.
engineering_relevance: The approach offers a practical route to simulate magnesium compounds at larger length and time scales than DFT, with potential value for adsorption, dynamics, phonons, and charge-transfer materials modeling.
method: Combine DFTB with a MACE machine-learning interatomic potential.
Train MACE on the DFT–DFTB energy and force residuals, excluding the pair-potential term.
Apply the model to structural relaxation, molecular dynamics, and phonon calculations for MgO-based systems.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports tests on MgO–CO2 adsorption, water-cluster dynamics, and fcc/bcc MgO phonons, showing improved accuracy over DFTB with a pair potential and comparison against DFT and pure MACE.
watch_item: The original MACE training set may not cover the full potential-energy surface, so accuracy could degrade in poorly represented regions unless the dataset is expanded.
paper_title: A Combined Tight Binding with Machine Learning Potential Model for Magnesium Compounds
